title: elaeocarpus habbemensis subsp. habbemensis
intro: Elaeocarpus habbemensis subsp. habbemensis, also known as the Papuan blueberry tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Elaeocarpaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in the Pacific region for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which are derived from its leaves and fruits. The health benefits of Elaeocarpus habbemensis subsp. habbemensis include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and prevent infections. Herbal preparations of this plant are typically made by infusing its leaves and fruits in water to create a tea or tincture that can be used to treat various health conditions.
